provisions.title: Citation
provisions.content:
1. Citation
These Regulations may be cited as the Botswana Tourism Organisation Regulations.

provisions.title: Form of application for grading
provisions.content:
2. Form of application for grading
An application made under section 26 of the Act, for a grade, shall be in the forms set out in Part A of Schedule I.

provisions.title: Issue of grading certificate plaque
provisions.content:
3. Issue of grading certificate plaque
(1) Botswana Tourism shall grade the tourist enterprise in accordance with the grades specified in Part A of Schedule II.
(2) Where a tourist enterprise meets the terms and conditions of section 27 of the Act, Botswana Tourism shall, on payment of the fees set out in Schedule III, issue a grading certificate set out in Part B of Schedule II.
(3) Botswana Tourism shall issue a plaque depicting the name of the tourist enterprise and the grade assigned to it under subregulation (2).

provisions.title: Conditions of grading certificate
provisions.content:
4. Conditions of grading certificate
(1) A tourist enterprise shall operate in accordance with the terms and conditions stipulated in the grading certificate issued under regulation 3.
(2) A grading certificate shall be granted to a tourist enterprise licensed under the Tourism Act (Cap. 42:09).

provisions.title: Application for renewal of grading certificate
provisions.content:
5. Application for renewal of grading certificate
(1) An application under section 28(2) of the Act, for renewal of a grading certificate, shall be in the form set out in Part B of Schedule I.
(2) On an application for renewal under this regulation, the applicant shall pay the grading fees set out in Schedule III.

provisions.title: Application for amendment of grade
provisions.content:
6. Application for amendment of grade
(1) An application under section 29(1) of the Act, for amendment of a grade, shall be in the form set out in Part C of Schedule I.
(2) On an application for amendment under this regulation, the applicant shall pay a non-refundable fee of P1 000.
